1	---
2	title: DDx Skills
3	weight: 7
4	---
5	
6	DDx ships 6 built-in skills that agents discover as slash commands. These
7	guide agents through DDx operations instead of requiring them to memorize
8	flag combinations.
9	
10	## Available Skills
11	
12	| Skill | Description |
13	|-------|-------------|
14	| `/ddx-bead` | Create and manage beads with proper metadata |
15	| `/ddx-agent` | Select harness, model, and effort for agent dispatch |
16	| `/ddx-install` | Search, preview, and install packages |
17	| `/ddx-status` | Project health overview |
18	| `/ddx-review` | Multi-agent quorum and fresh-eyes code review |
19	| `/ddx-run` | Execute a bead end-to-end: claim → build → verify → close |
20	
21	Skills are installed automatically by `ddx init` to the project's
22	`.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` directories as real files.
23	
24	## `/ddx-bead` — Create Work Items
25	
26	Instead of remembering flags, invoke the skill:
27	
28	```
29	/ddx-bead "Implement user login"
30	```
31	
32	The skill guides the agent through:
33	- Choosing type (task, epic, bug, chore)
34	- Setting labels and spec-id
35	- Writing acceptance criteria
36	- Wiring dependencies
37	
38	## `/ddx-review` — Multi-Agent Review
39	
40	Run a structured code review across multiple harnesses:
41	
42	```
43	/ddx-review
44	```
45	
46	The skill:
47	1. Checks available harnesses via `ddx agent list`
48	2. Assembles a review prompt with relevant context
49	3. Dispatches via `ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude`
50	4. Reports where models agree and disagree
51	
52	### Example: Review a bead's implementation
53	
54	```
55	/ddx-review bead ddx-abc123
56	```
57	
58	The skill reads the bead's spec-id and acceptance criteria, then reviews
59	the implementation against them.
60	
61	## `/ddx-run` — Execute a Bead
62	
63	The full lifecycle for implementing a work item:
64	
65	```
66	/ddx-run ddx-abc123
67	```
68	
69	The skill:
70	1. Reads the bead's spec-id and acceptance criteria
71	2. Loads the governing artifact
72	3. Claims the bead (`ddx bead update <id> --claim`)
73	4. Dispatches an agent with full context
74	5. Verifies tests pass after implementation
75	6. Closes the bead if acceptance criteria are met
76	
77	This prevents agents from skipping verification or claiming completion
78	without running tests.
79	
80	## `/ddx-agent` — Guided Dispatch
81	
82	When you need to run an agent but aren't sure which model or effort level:
83	
84	```
85	/ddx-agent
86	```
87	
88	Shows available harnesses, their capabilities, and helps select the right
89	configuration before dispatching.
90	
91	## `/ddx-status` — Health Check
92	
93	Quick overview of project state:
94	
95	```
96	/ddx-status
97	```
98	
99	Runs `ddx doctor`, `ddx bead list`, and `ddx bead ready` and summarizes
100	the findings.
101	
102	## Creating Custom Skills
103	
104	See [Creating Plugins](../plugins) for how to add your own skills to DDx
105	or distribute them as a plugin.
106

1	---
2	name: ddx
3	description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
4	---
5	
6	# DDx
7	
8	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
9	document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
10	items with acceptance criteria), an agent service boundary (DDx
11	orchestrates work while the upstream agent routes providers/models), a persona system (bindable AI
12	personalities), a library registry (plugins with prompts, templates,
13	personas), and git-aware synchronization. This skill makes any
14	skills-compatible coding agent (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini
15	CLI, etc.) understand and operate the DDx surface correctly.
16	
17	## How this skill works
18	
19	The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
20	router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.
21	
22	**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
23	matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
24	not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
25	guidance, not this overview alone.**
26	
27	## Vocabulary
28	
29	Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
30	exact definitions.
31	
32	- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
33	  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
34	- **Queue** — the set of open beads. The *ready queue* is the subset
35	  with all dependencies closed. `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`.
36	- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
37	  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
38	- **Blocked** — a bead with at least one unclosed dependency.
39	  `ddx bead blocked`.
40	- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
41	  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
42	- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
43	  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
44	  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
45	- **Run** — one agent invocation atom. `ddx run` calls upstream agent
46	  `Execute` once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
47	  passthrough constraints.
48	- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
49	  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
50	  finalization.
51	- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
52	  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
53	- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
54	  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
55	- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
56	  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
57	  below).
58	- **Harness** — an upstream agent routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
59	  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
60	  branch on harness names.
61	- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
62	  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
63	  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
64	- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
65	  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
66	  personas.
67	- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
68	  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
69	- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
70	  upstream agent. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries; the agent owns
71	  the concrete harness/provider/model routing.
72	- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
73	  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
74	  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
75	  `ddx install <name>`.
76	- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
77	  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
78	  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
79	- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
80	  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
81	  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
82	  orchestration; that remains harness business.
83	- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
84	  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
85	- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
86	  `ddx upgrade`.
87	- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
88	  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
89	  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
90	  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
91	- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
92	  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
93	  via `spec-id`.
94	- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
95	  at its governing artifact.
96	
97	## Intent router
98	
99	Before responding, read the matching file.
100	
101	| User says / asks about | Read this file |
102	|---|---|
103	| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
104	| "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "run work", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
105	| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
106	| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
107	| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |
108	
109	If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
110	run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
111	user which concept they mean rather than guessing.
112	
113	## Top-level policy reminders
114	
115	These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
116	reference file; do not violate them.
117	
118	- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
119	  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
120	  bead history and cannot be audited.
121	- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
122	  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
123	  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
124	  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
125	- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
126	  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
127	  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
128	  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
129	  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
130	  branches.
131	- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
132	  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
133	  its own isolated checkout. Execute-bead does this automatically;
134	  manual parallel work should too.
135	- **Power-first agent dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
136	  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
137	  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.
138	
139	## Links out
140	
141	- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
142	- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
143	  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
144	  executions, and skills features.
145	- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
146	  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
147	- Open standard this skill conforms to:
148	  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).
149

input.content:
---
title: DDx Skill
weight: 7
---

DDx ships a single built-in skill, `ddx`, that any
[agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io)-compatible coding agent (Claude Code,
OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) discovers and uses to operate the DDx surface
correctly. The skill is installed automatically by `ddx init` to the project's
`.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` directories as real files.

## Structure

The skill follows the standard SKILL.md + `reference/` layout. The top-level
`SKILL.md` is an overview and intent router; the real domain guidance lives in
`reference/*.md` files that the agent reads on demand.

```
ddx/
  SKILL.md          # overview, vocabulary, intent router
  reference/
    beads.md        # work-item authoring
    work.md         # draining the queue, executing beads
    review.md       # bead review, quorum/adversarial review
    agents.md       # agent dispatch, harness/power passthrough, personas
    status.md       # queue health, ddx doctor, sync status
```

## Reference areas

The skill routes the agent to one of these reference files based on the user's
intent:

### beads

Authoring and managing work items: `ddx bead create`, metadata, labels,
spec-id, acceptance criteria, dependency edges.

### work

Draining the queue end-to-end: `ddx work` to pick ready beads, `ddx try` to
attempt one bead in an isolated worktree, claim → build → verify → close.

### review

Two concepts under one roof. *Bead review* (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a
completed bead against its acceptance criteria. *Comparison/adversarial
review* composes multiple `ddx run` invocations to cross-check work.

### agents

Dispatching agents via `ddx run`: power bounds (`--min-power` / `--max-power`),
harness/provider/model passthrough constraints, and persona bindings (`ddx
persona list/show/bind`).

### status

Queue and project health: `ddx doctor`, `ddx bead ready`, `ddx bead blocked`,
sync status, and "how am I doing" overviews.

## Creating Custom Skills

Plugins can ship additional skills alongside the built-in `ddx` skill. See
[Creating Plugins](../plugins) for how to add your own skills or distribute
them as a plugin.
